summary: Excellent audience recording of a legendary 2/26/77 show featuring a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequence and an epic Terrapin opener.
review: The audience tape captures the raw energy of the February‑1977 performance with crystal‑clear ambience, making it one of the finest matrix/audience recordings in the Archive. Reviewers rave about the blistering second set—Help on the Way into Slipknot! and Franklin’s Tower, the powerful Blues for Allah suite, and a thunderous Around & Around—while noting only minor lyrical slips in Franklin’s Tower.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high 5‑star reviews praise both the musical performance and the audio quality; weighting yields an almost perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe the tape as crisp, clear, and one of the finest audience/matrix recordings. Some note streamed versions sound slightly crisper, but overall the ambience and instrument separation are superb.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with a historic Terrapin Station and Estimated Prophet, builds momentum through the first set, then delivers an explosive second set highlighted by Help‑Slip‑Frank sequence and a ferocious Around & Around, keeping the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praise Jerry's tone on Help > Slip > Franklin’s, calling it perfect and lilted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil's bass is highlighted as intense and a key driver of the show's power, especially during the Help sequence.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob gets a shout‑out with "Hey Bob" and Mexicali Blues, but no detailed critique of his playing.
